2026-04-09T03:17:05.4559369Z Filed a GitHub issue documenting the CHANGELOG gap for `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent`. The PR #57631 (migration to new TypeSpec emitter) introduced significant changes — new backward-compat shims, renamed client builder extension, new model factory, and a metadata type change — but the `1.2.0-beta.11 (Unreleased)` CHANGELOG section remained completely empty. The issue includes a full implementation guide for a coding agent to populate the CHANGELOG correctly. Triage dispatched.

2026-04-09T03:17:09.3071929Z   
2026-04-09T03:17:09.3072188Z   Key observations:
2026-04-09T03:17:09.3072688Z   1. The generated code was completely replaced (many files removed and added)
2026-04-09T03:17:09.3073425Z   2. New backward compatibility shims were added in `BackwardCompat/` folder
2026-04-09T03:17:09.3074270Z   3. The API surface changed significantly (970 additions, 145 deletions in the API file)
2026-04-09T03:17:09.3075253Z   4. New class `AgentsPersistentModelFactory` was added
2026-04-09T03:17:09.3076413Z   5. New client builder extensions were renamed from `AIAgentsPersistentClientBuilderExtensions` to `AgentsPersistentClientBuilderExtensions`
2026-04-09T03:17:09.3077409Z   
2026-04-09T03:17:09.3077660Z   
2026-04-09T03:17:09.3078177Z   6. A `MultipartFormDataRequestContent` class was introduced to handle multipart form data
2026-04-09T03:17:09.3078853Z

2026-04-09T03:17:09.3092984Z   
2026-04-09T03:17:09.3093766Z   2. **README.md**: The README seems fairly comprehensive but:
2026-04-09T03:17:09.3094469Z      - It doesn't mention the migration to the new TypeSpec emitter
2026-04-09T03:17:09.3095527Z      - The new `BackwardCompat` shims for upgrading from older versions aren't documented anywhere
2026-04-09T03:17:09.3096500Z      - The renamed client builder extension isn't mentioned
2026-04-09T03:17:09.3097002Z   
2026-04-09T03:17:09.3099722Z   The biggest gap is that PR #57631, which is a major migration involving the TypeSpec emitter transition, backward compatibility shims across multiple files, and the client builder extension rename, has no CHANGELOG entries at all.  The API surface saw significant changes with 970 additions and 145 deletions, plus a new model factory class, but none of this is reflected in the 1.2.0-beta.11 release notes. I should file an issue to get this documented.
2026-04-09T03:17:09.3102332Z

2026-04-09T03:17:09.3105263Z   
2026-04-09T03:17:09.3106238Z   1. **CHANGELOG.md for 1.2.0-beta.11 (Unreleased) is empty** - This is the most critical gap. The migration from the old TypeSpec emitter to the new one introduced:
2026-04-09T03:17:09.3108013Z      - New `BackwardCompat` shims: `AbstractTypeConstructors.cs`, `ClientMethodShims.cs`, `MissingTypes.cs`, `ModelFactoryShims.cs`, `PropertyShims.cs`
2026-04-09T03:17:09.3109821Z      - These are backward compatibility shims that changed metadata parameters from `IReadOnlyDictionary<string,string>` to `IDictionary<string,string>` 
2026-04-09T03:17:09.3111355Z      - Renamed `AIAgentsPersistentClientBuilderExtensions` to `AgentsPersistentClientBuilderExtensions`
2026-04-09T03:17:09.3112322Z      - Added new `AgentsPersistentModelFactory` class
2026-04-09T03:17:09.3113038Z      - Significant API surface changes (970 additions, 145 deletions in the API file)
2026-04-09T03:17:09.3113891Z      - The migration involved a complete regeneration of all Generated/ files
2026-04-09T03:17:09.3114494Z   
2026-04-09T03:17:09.3116289Z   2. The README itself looks comprehensive, so no major README gaps. But the CHANGELOG needs updating for this migration.
2026-04-09T03:17:09.3116974Z   
2026-04-09T03:17:09.3117603Z   The key documentation gap here is the CHANGELOG not capturing the significant internal refactoring, backward compat shims, and the migration to the new TypeSpec emitter.
2026-04-09T03:17:09.3118272Z

2026-04-09T03:17:09.5749267Z [36;1m# AWF runs with sudo, creating files owned by root[0m
2026-04-09T03:17:09.5749472Z [36;1msudo chmod -R a+r /tmp/gh-aw/sandbox/firewall/logs 2>/dev/null || true[0m
2026-04-09T03:17:09.5749794Z [36;1m# Only run awf logs summary if awf command exists (it may not be installed if workflow failed before install step)[0m
